description: RGB shows the Lahaina coastal town with a large dark brown/grey area inland matching the burned district, clearly distinct from surrounding dry reddish terrain. SWIR confirms an extensive burn scar as a dark brown mass, with no active fire glow — consistent with post-fire aftermath roughly one week after peak.
labeler_notes: Burn scar footprint is large and unambiguous in both views. No active fire signal in SWIR — aftermath only, so HIGH not CRITICAL.

description: RGB shows dry upland agricultural and scrub terrain with cloud cover obscuring the right half of the frame; no smoke, burn scar, or fire signal visible in the exposed areas. SWIR shows bright green healthy vegetation on the cloud-free left portion and orange/tan tones for dry scrub — no hazard signature.
labeler_notes: Tile is offset from the Lahaina burn area; right-half cloud cover limits interpretation but the exposed terrain shows no hazard.

description: RGB shows the same Lahaina coastal scene as the Aug 17 capture; the burned town area remains visible as a distinctive dark brownish-grey patch along the coastal strip, contrasting with surrounding dry land. SWIR confirms the persistent burn scar as a dark brown inland area with healthy vegetation signatures only at the margins.
labeler_notes: Burn scar still clearly visible three weeks post-peak; useful as a confirmed aftermath example. Very similar to o0 Aug 17 tile.

description: RGB shows upland mixed agricultural and scrub terrain with partial cloud cover on the right side; the exposed left portion is brown/dry but shows no burn scar or smoke. SWIR displays bright green (healthy vegetation) on the right and orange/tan (dry scrub) on the left with no burn or fire signal.
labeler_notes: Consistent with the Aug 17 o1 tile — offset from burn area, cloud-partially obscured, no hazard visible.

description: RGB shows Kelowna city on the west shore of Okanagan Lake under clear skies with no smoke, fire, or anomaly visible. SWIR shows healthy bright green vegetation in residential and park areas with urban magenta/pink tones — entirely normal urban scene, no hazard evidence.
labeler_notes: Pre-peak capture (event peak Aug 18); clean baseline urban scene.

description: RGB shows a hillside residential and forested area north of Kelowna under clear skies; terrain is mixed green and brown (typical summer dry conditions) with no smoke or burn scar. SWIR shows a mix of healthy green vegetation and orange/tan tones indicating dry but unstressed scrub — no fire signal present.
labeler_notes: Pre-peak capture; orange SWIR tones reflect dry summer conditions not a hazard. Worth noting as a pre-event baseline for the hillside tile.

description: RGB shows the Kelowna city lakefront on the peak fire date with clear skies and no visible smoke or burn in this tile; the urban grid and waterfront are intact. SWIR confirms healthy green vegetation throughout with normal urban signatures — no fire or smoke encroachment visible in this particular offset.
labeler_notes: Fire peak date but this city-center tile shows no hazard; smoke/fire activity was to the north of this footprint (visible in o1).

description: RGB shows a dense smoke plume billowing over the hillside north of Kelowna with a visible dark burn scar on the terrain below the smoke; the hillside shows striated brown burn patterns cutting through residential streets. SWIR shows orange/tan burn-affected vegetation across the hillside and a bright region beneath the smoke consistent with an active or very recent fire front.
labeler_notes: Smoke partially obscures the burn area but active fire evidence is strong enough for CRITICAL. This is the standout high-value capture in the batch — peak fire day, active smoke plume, confirmed burn on hillside.

description: RGB is almost entirely obscured by thick cloud cover; only a small fragment of terrain is visible in the lower-left corner. SWIR likewise shows a blue-green cloud signal with no usable ground information.
labeler_notes: Greater than 90% cloud cover — ground is not interpretable.

description: RGB is essentially a white-out — dense cloud fills over 95% of the frame with only a tiny sliver of terrain at the far right edge. SWIR is uniformly cyan/cloud throughout with no ground signal.
labeler_notes: Completely cloud-obscured; no useful information can be extracted.

description: RGB shows Kelowna urban grid along Okanagan Lake with a bridge and mixed suburban/agricultural land; SWIR shows uniform healthy green vegetation throughout with no burn scar or thermal anomaly visible.
labeler_notes: Clean urban tile well past event window — no wildfire aftermath detectable in this footprint.

description: RGB shows a dark brown corridor running along the hillside terrain northeast of the lake, consistent with a burn scar; SWIR confirms with a distinct brownish-tan band of reduced vegetation reflectance flanked by healthier green zones.
labeler_notes: Burn scar signature is spatially coherent in both views — well-defined corridor matches post-wildfire aftermath.

description: RGB shows rugged dark forest/scrub terrain with a cloud band across the upper portion partially obscuring the scene; SWIR reveals mostly green healthy vegetation with some yellow-orange stressed patches in the lower right but no clear burn scar.
labeler_notes: Pre-event capture; cloud at top limits upper portion. Stressed patches in SWIR lower right are modest and insufficient to confirm hazard.

description: RGB is dominated by thick white cloud cover occupying roughly 80% of the frame with only narrow dark fringe at corners; SWIR similarly cloud-saturated with almost no ground signal.
labeler_notes: Insufficient ground visibility in both bands to make any assessment.

description: RGB shows a uniformly dark/hazy brown cast over dense terrain with a visible road network, consistent with heavy smoke overlay during or immediately after fire activity; SWIR penetrates haze to show stressed yellow-orange vegetation in the upper portion and mixed green below, confirming elevated vegetation stress across the scene.
labeler_notes: Smoke/haze in RGB substantially degrades optical clarity; SWIR smoke penetration reveals widespread vegetation stress matching peak-event timing.

description: RGB shows dry brownish-tan terrain with scattered green patches and urban areas — typical dry scrubland or light post-fire mix; SWIR shows predominantly yellow-green tones with orange-brown patches indicating stressed or dry vegetation but no dominant burn scar signature.
labeler_notes: Stress signal present in SWIR but not at the level or spatial pattern expected for a confirmed burn scar; scene reads as dry landscape or marginal stress.

description: RGB shows a large dark grey zone covering most of the frame with residual smoke visible at the lower-right corner and brown terrain at the top edge; SWIR confirms an extensive dark brown/black region across the lower two-thirds of the scene, a clear burn scar signature nine days after the event peak.
labeler_notes: Smoke and residual haze reduce RGB clarity but SWIR unambiguously shows large-scale burn scar — would escalate to CRITICAL if active fire glow were confirmed, but only scar and smoke are visible.

description: RGB shows hazy grey terrain with thin cloud/smoke streaks and some brown patchy areas; SWIR shows mixed yellow-green with a narrow brownish strip at the lower edge and some orange-yellow stressed zones but no widespread dark burn scar.
labeler_notes: Haze and thin cloud limit confidence; limited stressed vegetation visible in SWIR but no clear burn scar extent to confirm hazard.

description: RGB is dominated by thick white cloud cover filling the upper 70% of the frame with only a narrow dark strip at the bottom; SWIR similarly cloud-dominated with only a small brownish-orange fringe at the bottom that may indicate burn scar remnant.
labeler_notes: Cloud covers too much of the scene for reliable interpretation; the small ground strip at bottom is insufficient to assign a confident hazard label.

description: RGB shows urban area in the upper third with partial cloud patches across the lower scene and dark terrain patches visible through gaps; SWIR shows orange-yellow urban signature at top and brownish patches consistent with burn scar remnant visible through cloud gaps in the lower portion.
labeler_notes: Enough ground is visible through cloud gaps in SWIR to identify burn scar aftermath; cloud limits confidence but does not fully obstruct the scene.

description: RGB shows dense coastal city with healthy vegetation on hillsides and no smoke or discoloration. SWIR shows the expected pink/magenta urban signature and bright-green vegetation patches, with no fire glow or burn-scar darkening.
labeler_notes: Pre-event baseline image; useful as negative example.

description: RGB is dominated by open ocean with only a tiny sliver of coastline in the bottom-right corner. SWIR is almost entirely black (ocean), confirming negligible land coverage.
labeler_notes: Offset tile falls mostly over water; very little informational value.

description: RGB shows the urban hillside area at the event-peak date with muted brownish tones in upper slopes but no clear smoke plume. SWIR displays the usual pink urban signature with no bright-red fire glow and no unambiguous dark burn scar visible at this resolution.
labeler_notes: Acquired on the reported event-peak date but active fire signal is not confirmed visually; could be partially recovered or fire outside tile.

description: RGB shows open ocean with three ships near the bottom-right corner; SWIR confirms the vessels as small bright specks with no thermal anomaly or slick signature around them.
labeler_notes: Ocean offset tile; ships are routine vessel traffic.

description: RGB reveals noticeably darker, brownish-grey patches on the upper hillsides consistent with burn scarring not present in earlier dates. SWIR shows those same areas as comparatively low-reflectance zones amid the surrounding pink urban/vegetation mix, supporting a burn-scar interpretation.
labeler_notes: Post-fire burn scar visible; no active fire glow present, placing this as HIGH (aftermath) rather than CRITICAL.

description: RGB shows a single red cargo ship on open ocean; SWIR shows a single faint bright spot at the same location with no thermal or slick anomaly.
labeler_notes: Ocean tile with routine vessel; no hazard signal.

description: RGB shows moderate-to-heavy cloud cover over the city and hillsides, limiting ground visibility to small gaps. SWIR confirms a thick cloud deck (bright white) over most of the scene with only fragments of urban terrain visible in the lower portion.
labeler_notes: Clouds obscure the burn-scar area of interest; not usable for hazard assessment.

description: RGB is almost entirely covered by dense cumulus cloud over the ocean tile. SWIR confirms near-total cloud cover with no ground signal discernible.
labeler_notes: Ocean tile fully obscured by cloud; no usable content.

description: RGB shows a dry coastal city on pale limestone with a visible dry wadi channel and no floodwater or damage; SWIR shows a yellow/cream urban signature and sparse green patches with no anomalous moisture or damage signal.
labeler_notes: Pre-flood baseline; wadi is completely dry two weeks before the catastrophic flood event.

description: RGB is entirely uniform dark navy with no discernible land content; SWIR is entirely black, confirming this tile is pure open ocean or a no-data region.
labeler_notes: No land content in frame; tile is unusable.

description: RGB shows coastal urban area with partial cloud and prominent brown sediment deposits covering roads and structures. SWIR reveals an extensive bright-blue inundation band along the entire coastline and into the city, confirming active widespread flooding.
labeler_notes: Partial cloud reduces coverage but flood signal is unambiguous in SWIR; sediment clearly visible in RGB along the wadi path.

description: RGB shows open ocean surface with scattered clouds and no discernible land. SWIR is predominantly dark water with no ground-level features visible.
labeler_notes: Tile falls over open sea; no terrain present to evaluate.

description: RGB is cloud-free and shows the coastal city with prominent brown sediment scar channels cutting through the urban grid, indicating recent flood scour. SWIR confirms urban fabric (bright yellow-white) with thin blue linear channels tracing the wadi path through the city — residual flood corridor still visible.
labeler_notes: Peak flood passed; post-event sediment scarring and channel erosion well documented in this clear acquisition ten days after peak.

description: RGB is almost entirely black with only faint teal gradients near the lower edge, consistent with a night-time or near-zero-reflectance acquisition. SWIR is uniformly black with no retrievable signal.
labeler_notes: Effectively a no-data tile; possibly night pass or severe underexposure.

description: RGB shows the coastal city clearly with some thin cloud and notably turbid (brown-green) nearshore water indicating sustained sediment discharge from flood-affected wadis. SWIR shows the urban area with green patches in surrounding fields and faint blue-toned linear channels through the city suggesting residual flood traces.
labeler_notes: One month post-peak; turbid plume still visible offshore and flood-scoured channels remain distinguishable — qualifies as HIGH aftermath.

description: RGB shows dark open ocean with scattered small cumulus clouds and no land visible. SWIR is nearly entirely black with only the bright cloud tops discernible.
labeler_notes: Ocean-only tile; no ground to assess.

description: RGB shows suburban and agricultural land on the left half with the right half obscured by cloud. SWIR confirms healthy bright-green vegetation on the visible left portion with no flood or fire signature; cloud dominates the right side.
labeler_notes: Pre-event baseline capture; cloud limits right-half interpretation but no hazard evidence in visible portion.

description: RGB is approximately 70% saturated white cloud with only the left strip of urban area visible. SWIR is similarly dominated by bright cloud returns with only a narrow left-edge strip showing green vegetation.
labeler_notes: Cloud cover is too extensive to draw any meaningful hazard conclusion; SKIP per policy.

description: RGB shows a mix of urban and agricultural terrain with pale, turbid water visible in the lower-right quadrant. SWIR reveals a large contiguous dark-blue region occupying roughly the right half of the frame, a definitive signal of widespread active inundation across agricultural and peri-urban land.
labeler_notes: Flood extent in SWIR is dramatic; the turbid RGB appearance corroborates fresh flood water with suspended sediment — high-priority downlink.

description: RGB shows an urban strip on the left with open, brown muddy terrain to the right and hints of standing water. SWIR displays an intensely dark-blue zone covering the majority of the right portion, confirming large-scale flood inundation abutting the urban edge.
labeler_notes: Flood water directly adjacent to urban area; clear threat to infrastructure visible in both channels.

description: RGB shows an urban/semi-urban scene with fields and roads; SWIR reveals a large, deep dark-blue region on the eastern side of the frame indicating extensive standing water consistent with active flooding.
labeler_notes: Floodwater signature in SWIR is unambiguous — dark blue covers roughly a third of the frame.

description: RGB shows a rural corridor with a road/channel and small settlement; SWIR shows large dark-blue water coverage dominating the right half and threading through linear channels, indicating widespread active flooding.
labeler_notes: Blue water signal in SWIR is extensive; consistent with peak flood or shortly after.

description: Both RGB and SWIR are entirely white with no ground visible; complete cloud cover prevents any surface interpretation.
labeler_notes: Total cloud cover — no usable signal in either band.

description: Both RGB and SWIR show near-total white cloud cover with only faint traces in SWIR; ground is fully obscured.
labeler_notes: Effectively same cloud situation as o0 for this timestamp.

description: RGB is dominated by white clouds with only a thin diagonal strip of dark ground visible at the lower portion; SWIR shows the same clouds as white with a narrow bright-green vegetation strip, too limited for hazard assessment.
labeler_notes: Cloud cover exceeds ~85% of frame; the small ground strip is insufficient for meaningful labeling.

description: RGB shows mostly white cloud cover with scattered dark wisps; SWIR similarly cloud-dominated with only small green ground patches, preventing confident surface assessment.
labeler_notes: Cloud fraction approximately 80-90%; only fragments of ground visible.

description: RGB shows a dense urban grid with dark surfaces and roads; SWIR shows a yellow-green and magenta pattern consistent with dense urban development, with no discernible dark water bodies indicating flood inundation.
labeler_notes: Clear cloud-free urban scene; no flood signature visible in SWIR at this timestamp despite event window.

description: RGB shows suburban/urban development in the lower half and forested area above; SWIR shows bright-green healthy vegetation and magenta urban grid with no significant dark water pooling visible.
labeler_notes: Cloud-free; SWIR does not reveal flood inundation in this tile at this date.

description: RGB has roughly 40-50% patchy cloud cover obscuring urban ground; SWIR similarly patchy with clouds and urban signal through gaps, materially limiting interpretation.
labeler_notes: Borderline cloud fraction; called SKIP because cloud patchwork prevents confident assessment of flood extent.

description: RGB shows ~50% patchy cloud cover with urban and vegetated patches visible through gaps; SWIR also cloud-patchy with green ground tones, insufficient for reliable hazard assessment.
labeler_notes: Similar cloud fraction to o0; ground visible but fragmented — too uncertain for a confident priority label.

description: RGB shows a dense urban core with road networks and building blocks; SWIR confirms the urban footprint in magenta/pink tones surrounded by healthy bright-green vegetation with no dark water pooling visible.
labeler_notes: Post-flood timestamp but no residual inundation detectable in either band; urban scene only.

description: RGB shows a forested hillside transitioning to a suburban fringe with dense green canopy dominating; SWIR confirms bright-green healthy vegetation across the frame with no dark water bodies or anomalous signatures.
labeler_notes: Well past the May 8 event peak; vegetation appears fully healthy with no flood aftermath detectable.

description: RGB shows a mid-size city surrounded by agricultural fields with a river at normal levels; SWIR shows yellow/orange-dominant agricultural land and pink urban area with no dark water pooling — a pre-flood baseline.
labeler_notes: Capture is one week before the Sep 8 flood peak; no hazard visible, useful as baseline reference.

description: RGB shows flat agricultural patchwork fields with a small settlement and a river at normal levels; SWIR shows a yellow/green field mosaic with healthy crops and no inundation signatures.
labeler_notes: Pre-event baseline for the agricultural tile; useful for change detection comparison against peak and recovery imagery.

description: RGB is nearly entirely black with only a thin white strip at top; SWIR similarly shows a thin light cyan strip and massive black no-data region covering ~90% of the frame.
labeler_notes: Acquisition at the flood peak is almost entirely obscured — likely cloud shadow + no-data artifact; not usable for labeling.

description: RGB is completely white indicating total cloud cover; SWIR is uniformly light cyan/blue confirming full cloud obscuration with no ground signal.
labeler_notes: 100% cloud cover on both bands; entirely unusable for hazard assessment.

description: RGB shows the city with visibly dark patches along the river corridor and in adjacent agricultural areas consistent with residual flooding; SWIR confirms dark blue/black water signatures in multiple areas flanking the urban zone.
labeler_notes: 12 days after the Sep 8 peak; clear residual floodwater still present in SWIR, makes this a strong HIGH for flood aftermath.

description: RGB shows agricultural fields with scattered dark wet patches and a small settlement; SWIR reveals dark blue/black pooling distributed across multiple field parcels and along the river margin confirming residual inundation.
labeler_notes: Agricultural tile shows clear multi-field inundation still present in SWIR 12 days post-peak; good HIGH training example.

description: RGB shows the city with a river that appears wider and darker than the pre-event baseline, suggesting elevated water levels; SWIR shows a dark blue water signature along the river and scattered dark patches in surrounding fields, indicating partial but incomplete flood recession.
labeler_notes: 37 days after peak, river and some periurban areas still show water presence in SWIR; borderline HIGH/MEDIUM — keeping HIGH given confirmed water above baseline.

description: RGB shows agricultural fields that are largely recovering but with some anomalous dark/wet-looking parcels; SWIR shows bright-green recovered vegetation dominating but with a distinct dark blue water body along the river margin and residual dark patches in low-lying field areas.
labeler_notes: Late recovery stage — most fields have recovered but river-adjacent areas retain water signal in SWIR; labeled HIGH due to confirmed residual water.

description: RGB shows approximately 95% black no-data fill with only a narrow strip of dark-green vegetation visible at the very bottom edge; SWIR is similarly dominated by black no-data with a thin bright-green strip at the bottom.
labeler_notes: Large black no-data region makes the frame unusable for hazard assessment.

description: RGB shows heavy cloud coverage across nearly the entire frame with only fragmentary glimpses of dark-green forest beneath; SWIR confirms cloud with bright-white areas and partial lime-green patches where vegetation is visible through gaps.
labeler_notes: Cloud cover obscures the vast majority of the ground surface.

description: RGB is almost entirely black no-data with a thin bottom strip showing vegetation and cloud edge; SWIR matches — black no-data dominating with a narrow bright-green and faint-orange strip at the bottom edge.
labeler_notes: No-data fill covers well over 90% of the frame; insufficient ground coverage to assess.

description: RGB shows largely clear-sky dark-green forested mountainous terrain with visible road/track lines and a small cloud patch in the lower left; SWIR is uniformly bright lime-green indicating healthy intact vegetation, with only scattered minor red dots and a dark diagonal drainage line — no fresh landslide scar or bare-earth exposure is visible.
labeler_notes: Capture is on the event-peak date but imagery shows intact forest with no confirmed disturbance scar; scattered SWIR red dots are minor anomalies only.

description: RGB frame is nearly all black no-data, with only a very thin strip along the bottom edge showing green vegetation canopy and a small cloud fragment; SWIR similarly shows black no-data dominating, with a narrow strip of bright-green and minor yellow-green at the bottom.
labeler_notes: No-data fill dominates; insufficient ground visible for meaningful assessment.

description: RGB shows significant cloud cover across the lower half and center with dark-green intact forest visible in the upper portion; SWIR reveals healthy bright-green vegetation above the cloud line with cyan-toned patches in the lower-left suggesting exposed wet ground or moisture anomaly, but cloud coverage prevents confident identification of a landslide scar.
labeler_notes: Partial cloud limits ground assessment of the areas most likely to show landslide aftermath; cyan SWIR patches worth flagging but not conclusive.

description: RGB is almost entirely black no-data with only a tiny cloud-edged strip visible at the very bottom; SWIR shows the same near-total black no-data fill with a narrow light-green strip at the bottom edge.
labeler_notes: Frame dominated by no-data; effectively unusable.

description: RGB is fully covered by thick white cloud with no ground visible; SWIR shows a uniform cyan and light-teal pattern across the entire frame, consistent with an optically thick cloud layer blocking all surface signal.
labeler_notes: Complete cloud cover — no ground information available.

description: RGB shows thin haze or cloud veil over terrain with a mottled blue-grey texture limiting ground clarity; SWIR reveals a striking spatial divide — the left half is bright lime-green indicating healthy vegetation, while the right half is strongly dark with a dense branching network pattern consistent with heavily disturbed, cleared, or landslide-affected terrain.
labeler_notes: Capture is 7 days before the event peak; the sharp SWIR contrast between intact vegetation and the dark branching zone on the right is a significant anomaly warranting HIGH priority. RGB haze reduces confidence.

description: RGB displays a uniform blue-grey hazy/cloud texture throughout the frame suggesting thin cloud or aerosol veil over the terrain; SWIR shows mottled dark-green and purple tones across the full frame indicating either cloud-covered disturbed terrain or stressed vegetation, with no clearly localized hazard signature or bare-earth exposure visible.
labeler_notes: Haze and possible cloud cover reduce interpretation confidence across the whole frame; no discrete hazard feature identifiable.

description: RGB shows coastal urban area with forest-covered hillside and partial cloud cover; SWIR reveals a distinct dark gray-brown elongated scar cutting through the bright green hillside behind the city, consistent with a fresh landslide exposing bare soil.
labeler_notes: Scar feature in SWIR is most diagnostic — dark irregular strip in hillside vegetation, matches event-peak date.

description: RGB is predominantly open ocean with a small vegetated island at the left edge; SWIR confirms dark water and bright green island vegetation with no anomalous offshore signature.
labeler_notes: Frame is almost entirely open water; minimal land content, no hazard signal visible.

description: RGB shows the same coastal urban-hillside area cleaner of cloud two weeks post-peak; SWIR retains a dark gray-brown elongated feature in the hillside consistent with a persistent landslide scar.
labeler_notes: Scar appears slightly smaller/less saturated than 2/22 but still clearly distinct from surrounding healthy green in SWIR.

description: RGB is dominated by dark open water with a small green island at the left; SWIR shows black water and bright green island with no anomalous surface features.
labeler_notes: Tiny bright dots at RGB right edge appear to be boats or sensor noise, not a hazard signature.

description: Both RGB and SWIR show a completely white frame with no visible ground — entire scene is obscured by thick cloud cover.
labeler_notes: No usable information in either band; full cloud cover.

description: RGB is near-white with near-total cloud cover; SWIR shows white cloud with faint cyan patches and no ground surface visible.
labeler_notes: Both bands cloud-saturated; ground completely uninterpretable.

description: RGB shows a healthy coastal town and dense green vegetation with minor cloud; SWIR confirms healthy bright-green vegetation and normal magenta urban tones with no offshore oil-slick signature visible in the water.
labeler_notes: Capture is before event peak; no oil visible. Labeled MEDIUM for coastal urban/port interest value.

description: RGB shows uniform dense green tropical forest with a small coastal strip and minor cloud patches; SWIR confirms predominantly healthy bright-green vegetation with no anomalous tones or offshore slick.
labeler_notes: Interior forest tile, pre-event capture, no hazard signal in either band.

description: RGB shows coastal urban area with dark streaky surface bands visible on the ocean offshore, distinct from normal open water; SWIR corroborates with an unusually dark and texturally smooth offshore area consistent with an oil slick suppressing sea-surface reflectance.
labeler_notes: Event-peak date; offshore dark linear/patchy features in RGB match SWIR darkening — consistent with surface oil slick. Confidence moderate; some cloud interference at frame edges.

description: RGB shows fragmented cloud cover dominating the frame with only small forest patches visible; SWIR confirms heavy cloud with bright white/yellow blooms obscuring the majority of the scene.
labeler_notes: Majority cloud cover in both bands prevents reliable assessment of oil spill or other surface hazard.

description: RGB shows a coastal shoreline with partial cloud cover and greenish-teal near-shore water with no obvious surface film or sheen; SWIR confirms water as dark/black and vegetation as bright green with no anomalous signatures in the water.
labeler_notes: Cloud patches reduce coverage of the offshore zone but enough coast is visible to assess; no oil slick signature detected.

description: RGB is dominated by thick cloud cover with only a small sliver of coastline visible in the lower portion; SWIR is similarly saturated and cloud-dominated, providing no usable view of the water surface.
labeler_notes: Ground obscured by heavy cloud in both bands; insufficient surface data to assess hazard.

description: RGB shows forested coast and settlement with significant cloud interruptions; SWIR shows vegetation as bright green and water as dark with no anomalous SWIR signal over the coastal water.
labeler_notes: Cloud cover limits full coverage of offshore zone; visible water areas show no oil signature in either band.

description: RGB is nearly entirely white, dominated by thick cloud with only trace fragments of land visible at the edges; SWIR is similarly saturated with cyan/white tones indicating extensive cloud, providing no usable ground data.
labeler_notes: Both bands effectively fully cloud-obscured; cannot assess surface conditions.

description: RGB shows a coastal industrial zone with scattered cloud and a distinctly dark elongated patch extending into the coastal water; SWIR shows an anomalous red/pink area over the coastal zone where RGB shows the dark water patch, consistent with a hydrocarbon or industrial surface film.
labeler_notes: Cloud patches reduce full coverage but the coastal anomaly is visible in a partially clear window; SWIR red signature over water warrants HIGH classification.

description: Both RGB and SWIR are essentially blank white with no visible ground features; the frame is completely cloud-covered or contains no-data fill.
labeler_notes: No usable imagery in either band; entirely obscured.

description: RGB is nearly all white with only a tiny fragment of ground visible at the bottom-left corner; SWIR is similarly blank and near-featureless, indicating near-total cloud or no-data coverage.
labeler_notes: Effectively no usable ground information in either band.

description: RGB shows arid inland terrain with a road corridor, sparse agricultural patches, and partial cloud at the top; SWIR shows predominantly pink/reddish tones indicating dry bare soil across the scene with no fire, flooding, or oil-related anomaly.
labeler_notes: Inland arid scene away from the coast; no hazard evidence in either band.

description: RGB shows a clear, cloud-free view of the Peruvian coastal city and adjacent dark ocean water with a sharp shoreline contrast; SWIR shows the ocean uniformly dark and the urban/bare terrain as pink/magenta with no anomalous red or film-like signature over the water.
labeler_notes: Good quality image of the spill area taken after the event peak, but neither band shows a confirmable surface oil signature; downlinkable for monitoring context.

description: RGB shows dry inland urban and suburban terrain with roads, sparse green patches, and extensive bare sandy ground; SWIR shows yellow/cream and pink-red tones consistent with dry soil and minimal vegetation, with no hazard indicators.
labeler_notes: Inland scene well away from the coast and spill zone; no hazard evidence.

description: RGB shows a normal-looking arid coastline with standard teal ocean; SWIR reveals a distinct bright cyan anomaly along the nearshore water surface where clean ocean is uniformly dark, consistent with a residual hydrocarbon slick on the water.
labeler_notes: Oil slick signature clearer in SWIR than RGB; extent is moderate, suggesting post-peak residual slick rather than an active large release.

description: RGB shows arid peri-urban terrain with sparse settlement and a small irrigated patch; SWIR confirms dry bare soil (magenta-pink) with healthy irrigated vegetation (yellow-green) and no anomalous signatures.
labeler_notes: Inland tile from the same event window; no oil, fire, flood, or stress signal visible in either band. Routine arid urban-fringe scene.
